reasoning: The Wikipedia table currently has a blank field for a Botulism vaccine. The recent literature (as of early 2025) confirms there are no FDA-approved vaccines for botulism, with some candidates still in development or early testing phases. Developing, testing, and approving a vaccine typically takes years, and botulism is not currently an acute global pandemic that would see a vaccine fast-tracked in the span of 1 to 6 months. The only real pathway to a "Yes" resolution would be an editor erroneously adding a candidate or historically discontinued IND vaccine to the Wikipedia page and that edit surviving until the resolution date. Given the high profile of medical pages on Wikipedia and the speed at which incorrect edits are typically reverted, this is highly unlikely. The probabilities start at the minimum allowed limit (0.05) and increase slightly over longer horizons to account for unexpected developments or sustained Wikipedia edits.
